title: Benzoyl-m-nitroacetanilide as a Gravimetric Reagent for Mercury (II)
author: NIRMALKUSUM CHAUDHURI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Burdwan University, Burdwan, West Bengal 

  Manuscript received 18 January 1973; accepted 26 May 1973  

 Benzoyl-m-nitroacetanilide has been used to determine 12 to 32 mg of mercury(II) gravixnetri­cally. The complex is precipitates quantitatively within the pH range, 2.5 to 9.0. Mercury(H) has b een separated from Cu 2 +, Pb 2 +, Co 2 +, Ni 2 +, Mn 2+ , Bi 3 +, Al 3 +, Fe 3 +, UO 2  2+  or WO 4  2-  using citrate, tartrate or fluoride as masking agent. However, A.g+, Ti+ or Cd 2 + does not precipitate under the conditions Hg 2 + does. The precipitate is weighed after drying as Hg (C 15 H 11 O 1 N 2 ) 2  containing 26.19% of mercury as it is stable up to 220°. The standard deviation is ±0.13%.

  Manuscript received 18 January 1973; accepted 26 May 1973  

 Benzoyl-m-nitroacetanilide has been used to determine 12 to 32 mg of mercury(II) gravixnetri­cally. The complex is precipitates quantitatively within the pH range, 2.5 to 9.0. Mercury(H) has b een separated from Cu 2 +, Pb 2 +, Co 2 +, Ni 2 +, Mn 2+ , Bi 3 +, Al 3 +, Fe 3 +, UO 2  2+  or WO 4  2-  using citrate, tartrate or fluoride as masking agent. However, A.g+, Ti+ or Cd 2 + does not precipitate under the conditions Hg 2 + does. The precipitate is weighed after drying as Hg (C 15 H 11 O 1 N 2 ) 2  containing 26.19% of mercury as it is stable up to 220°. The standard deviation is ±0.13%.
